commands.review_summary:
This PR adds native iOS ElevenLabs realtime and batch STT clients, streams mic PCM to ElevenLabs, wires TalkModeManager and Settings STT selectors, and adds Swift source-list entries.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR review blockers from source inspection: PR head shows the fixed 300 ms PTT wait, optimistic ready flag, fallback without Speech permission, and PTT error recovery routed through a continuous-only restart helper. No live iOS runtime proof is attached.
commands.review_followup: Manual maintainer review is needed because the branch changes iOS Talk architecture/privacy and lacks external real-device proof; automation should not repair it until direction is approved.

commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Channels-table merge helper that synthesizes missing `openclaw status` rows from the existing `channels.status` gateway snapshot, wires it into status overview collection, adds regression tests, and updates the changelog.

Reproducibility: yes. at the source-seam level: current main queries `channels.status` but returns only `buildChannelsTable` rows, and the linked report shows a non-empty channel status snapshot with an empty status table. I did not run a live configured-channel repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor-supplied non-empty real behavior proof and normal maintainer review; there is no narrow automated repair to queue from this review.

commands.review_summary:
The PR makes `getStatusSummary()` reconcile inspectable tasks once, passes that snapshot to task registry and audit summaries, and adds regression tests plus a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main calls both task summary helpers from `getStatusSummary()`, and each helper calls `reconcileInspectableTasks()` before reaching the clone-and-sort `listTaskRecords()` path. I did not run a live 10K-task benchmark in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No repair lane is needed because the branch already contains the focused fix; the remaining blocker is maintainer/CI handling of the failing security audit and queued exact-head checks.
